goal: Port structural.complexity.cognitive to v2: Structure.cognitive(c) view method (Campbell 2018) + run_cognitive_v2 + multi-language parity tests; retire legacy_v2_shim wrapper

summary.title: Port structural.complexity.cognitive to v2 (view-native)
summary.what: Added Structure.cognitive(c) implementing Campbell 2018 Cognitive Complexity. Iterative stack walk with depth tracking + boolean-op sequence collapsing via tree-sitter's node.parent. Consumes the per-grammar vocabulary from Language (decision_nodes, nesting_nodes, compensating_decisions, boolean_op_node, boolean_op_operators, definition_unwrap_types, callable). run_cognitive_v2 is a pure threshold check; the registry now wires run= directly instead of legacy_v2_shim wrapper. Parity verified against legacy ccx_kernel across all 11 supported languages + 6 Python cases (zero mismatches). 18 new tests; 504 tests pass total.
summary.why: The cognitive port is the natural follow-up to the cyclomatic v2 port: same kernel family, same per-language vocabulary already on Language (landed in the multi-language CCX intent), same walker pattern. Retiring the legacy_v2_shim wrapper for cognitive is one concrete step toward eventual retirement of _structural/ccx.py entirely.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.complexity.cognitive to v2: Structure.cognitive(c) view method (Campbell 2018) + run_cognitive_v2 + multi-language parity tests; retire legacy_v2_shim wrapper

decisions.chose: Iterative stack walk with (node, depth) tuples, matching the cyclomatic walker shape.
decisions.rationale: Recursive walk would be a hair more readable but mixing styles between cyclomatic (iterative) and cognitive (recursive) for the same logical operation would be incoherent. Both walkers in view.py now share the same shape.

decisions.chose: Use tree-sitter's node.parent for boolean-op sequence collapsing, not a threaded parent_op parameter.
decisions.rationale: tree-sitter exposes node.parent for free. Threading parent_bool_op_text through the stack would require duplicating parent inspection logic that the AST already provides. The legacy walker uses node.parent the same way.

decisions.chose: Extract _bool_op_text as a separate helper; refactor _bool_op_matches to delegate to it.
decisions.rationale: cognitive needs operator TEXT (to compare with parent's op text), cyclomatic needs only a match check. Sharing the extraction logic in one place is cheaper than duplicating it; the match check becomes a one-liner.

decisions.chose: Run the full polyglot parity script against legacy ccx_kernel as part of validation, beyond unit tests.
decisions.rationale: Hand-computed expectations across 11 languages would miss subtle grammar quirks (Ruby's extra decision node, JS treating for-of as for_in_statement). Cross-checking against legacy on a sweep corpus catches these; the unit tests then lock down the verified values.

decisions.chose: Document the Tree qualname-collision observation in the commit message, file as follow-up, do NOT fix in this intent.
decisions.rationale: Discovered during cross-file parity testing — Tree._node_by_qualname indexes by bare qualname (e.g. 'a.f') which collides when multiple files share the file-stem. Out of scope for cognitive; fixing it would mean re-keying the indexes and updating every consumer (cyclomatic, cognitive, future view methods).

review_notes: Parity vs legacy ccx_kernel.cog verified on a 16-callable polyglot fixture (Python linear/single-if/if-in-loop/nested/chain/mixed + 10 languages × `if (a && b) loop`). Zero mismatches.
18 new tests (504 total): 4 hand-computed Python canonical cases, 10 multi-language fixtures, 4 sequence-collapsing edge cases (Python and-chain, Python mixed, JS &&-chain, JS mixed).
Port status now: 3 view-native rules (cyclomatic, cognitive, imposters), 28 shim-wrapped. The npath port follows the same pattern next.
Tree qualname-collision (Tree._node_by_qualname keyed by bare qualname, not (path, qualname)) is the follow-up to file; affects ANY rule that iterates callables() across same-stem files.

architectural_claims: Cognitive complexity (Campbell 2018) is computed inside Structure.cognitive, consuming Language classmethods for per-grammar node sets. Rules consume the metric via the view; no AST walking in rules.
Cyclomatic and cognitive walkers share the same iterative shape (stack of (node, depth) tuples; per-language vocabulary lookup; nested-callable skip via lang.callable()).
Operator-text extraction is centralised in _bool_op_text; both _bool_op_matches and the cognitive sequence-collapse check delegate to it.
behavioral_changes: structural.complexity.cognitive rule runs view-native; identical CogC values to legacy across all 11 supported languages.
The run= slot for structural.complexity.cognitive is now run_cognitive_v2 (no shim); legacy run_cognitive remains in complexity.py but is no longer wired into the registry.
migration_notes: Tree._node_by_qualname collision bug noted for follow-up — affects any rule iterating callables() across same-file-stem corpora.
